title: validator-scan integration: per-row evidence verification
goal: Extend the existing validator-scan tooling so that for each PROVED PACKET_ACCEPTANCE_MATRIX HBR row it verifies the evidence_pointer actually resolves to a runnable proof artifact matching the rule's evidence_kind. A claimed-PROVED row whose evidence does not resolve must be flagged STEER with reason, blocking PASS closure per CX-503B1.
expected_diff_shape: New Node module .GOV/roles_shared/checks/hbr-validator-scan.mjs; integration hooks into existing validator-scan umbrella (a small shim or directly added sub-call); golden fixture exercising the four evidence_kind families.
unblock_conditions: MT-003 matrix-check is wired; validator-scan baseline exists in repo (confirm path before implementation; if absent, this MT also creates a minimal validator-scan entrypoint and documents the gap).
implementation_notes: Per the enforcement.policy of HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES.json: 'Validators must mark each row PROVED, NOT_APPLICABLE (with reason), or BLOCKED (with cause). PASS closure is illegal while any required HBR row is PENDING, STEER, or BLOCKED, per CX-503B1.' This MT adds the verification step. For each PROVED row, the scanner: (1) parses the evidence_pointer (supported formats: `test://<test_name>` resolves to a cargo test that must exist and have passed in the recorded run; `receipt://<receipt_id>` resolves to a RECEIPTS.jsonl row in the WP communication dir; `artifact://<path>` resolves to a file the validator can re-open and inspect; `event://<event_id>` resolves to an EventLedger row), (2) calls the kind-specific verifier (e.g., for `test_run_with_ledger_replay` the verifier asserts the test exists in the cargo test inventory and that its most recent recorded result is PASS; for `concurrent_session_test_run` the verifier additionally checks that the test name matches a pattern `*_concurrent_*` or carries a `#[concurrent]` attribute marker), (3) on mismatch downgrades the row to STEER with a typed reason. The downgrade is written back into the packet's matrix (with idempotent merge per MT-002 semantics). The CLI: `node .GOV/roles_shared/checks/hbr-validator-scan.mjs --packet <path>` exits 0 on success, 2 on any STEER/BLOCKED downgrade. Tests: fixture packet with a PROVED row pointing to a non-existent test must downgrade and exit 2; fixture pointing to an existing-passing test must pass.
risk_if_missed: Without per-row evidence verification, validators can mark rows PROVED based on prose; the matrix becomes a trust-me signal rather than a verifiable gate.
validator_focus: STEER downgrades are written back into the packet's matrix (not just logged); CLI exit codes match the documented contract; resolver supports all four pointer schemes in the test fixture.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Resolver must reject unknown URI schemes with an explicit error (no silent skip).
Downgrade writes back to packet.json atomically (temp file + rename) to avoid partial-write corruption.
Fixture coverage exercises all four pointer schemes: test://, receipt://, artifact://, event://.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: hbr-validator-scan.mjs supports the four required schemes (test://, receipt://, artifact://, event://), rejects unknown schemes with explicit reason, downgrades PROVED→STEER atomically via temp-file+rename per the red-team control, and walks active source files to verify test:// pointers. The repo-bound path check prevents artifact:// escape.

The repo-bound path check prevents artifact:// escape.
